evidence.card_row: Hover: `color/surface-2` (#F7F7F7) background shift <!-- shadow token not yet defined; use Tailwind default shadow-md until a token is added to the Elevation/Shadows section -->
evidence.elevation_note: <!-- Figma hover state for `Card` references a 'shadow' but no token is defined. Until a shadow scale is added here, downstream code should fall back to Tailwind's default `shadow-md` (or omit the shadow) and rely on the Card's `color/surface-2` background-shift for hover affordance. Do not invent shadow values inline. -->

resolutions.notes: Composer chose option (a)-equivalent: removed the dangling `shadow (token TBD)` clause from the Card States cell and replaced it with an explicit inline HTML comment instructing AI agents to fall back to Tailwind's default `shadow-md`. The Elevation/Shadows section retains its `_No elevation/shadow tokens detected._` line plus a clarifying HTML comment that forbids inline shadow invention. An AI agent reading the Card row will see a defined background-shift hover plus an explicit 'no token, use fallback' signal — no phantom token reference remains.

evidence.callout_marker: > ⚠ Naming/Role Conflict — Host Grotesk vs DM Sans
evidence.canonical_decision: local code wins; var(--font-sans) = Host Grotesk for body, var(--font-heading) = DM Sans for headings
dos_donts_added: Do: render all headings (<h1>-<h6>) with var(--font-heading) (DM Sans) and all body/UI text with var(--font-sans) (Host Grotesk)...
Don't: swap heading and body fonts to 'match Figma' without a corresponding update to apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx and this document's Typography callout...

resolutions.notes: Composer added a `> ⚠ Naming/Role Conflict` callout at the top of Typography that names both source artifacts, picks local-code as canonical (rendered site as source of truth, Figma as reference), updates the Font Families table header to `Role (canonical, local-code)`, and adds one paired Do/Don't entry referencing font roles. The decision is deterministic — an AI agent reading the file now has a single answer for which family renders <h1>. Fully satisfies the F-002 suggested fix.

conflict_handling.notes: Zero token-level conflicts in either inventory. The cross-source Typography role mismatch is now surfaced with a spec-shaped callout and resolved deterministically.
